# F1 · Algebra

> Chapter F1 Algebra — the opening chapter of Functional Programming in Elixir. Nine lessons showing that the algebra you know (functions, substitution, composition) is functional programming, with an algebra-to-Elixir mapping and an interactive function visualizer. In English.

Chapter map for LLMs/agents. Hub: [/elixir/algebra](/elixir/algebra). Links are clean root-relative URLs; this file lists every lesson in the chapter in course order.

## Lessons

- [What a function really is — F1.01](/elixir/algebra/functions): A function as a mapping: domain, codomain, and range; the single-output rule; and the first-class function as an ordinary Elixir value.
- [The substitution model — F1.02](/elixir/algebra/substitution): Equals for equals: evaluation by substitution, referential transparency, and the purity that makes a function safe to replace with its result.
- [Composition, f∘g — F1.03](/elixir/algebra/composition): Chaining functions into new ones: the composite g∘f, why order matters and grouping is free, and the pipe as composition written in evaluation order.
- [Immutability & binding — F1.04](/elixir/algebra/immutability): A name is a fixed value, not a box: binding versus rebinding, the match operator and the pin, and immutable data whose updates return new values.
- [Sets, sequences & mappings — F1.05](/elixir/algebra/collections): Three shapes of collection — ordered lists, distinct MapSets, key-to-value Maps — and Enum.map, the operation that applies a function across any of them.
- [Recursion & induction — F1.06](/elixir/algebra/recursion): Base case plus step: the call stack of a recursive sum, why the base case makes it terminate, and induction — the proof that shares recursion's shape.
- [Higher-order operators (Σ, Π) — F1.07](/elixir/algebra/higher-order): Σ and Π as operators over a function, the map / filter / reduce trio and how each reshapes a collection, and reduce as the general fold the others are instances of.
- [Equations & pattern matching — F1.08](/elixir/algebra/pattern-matching): Solving by structure: destructuring tuples, lists and maps, dispatching control by shape, and guards that refine a match by value.
- [Functions on the plane — F1.09](/elixir/algebra/plotting-lab): The F1 lab: a coordinate plotter for f, g, and their composites, with an x-trace that follows a value through f∘g — plotting as Enum.map, composition as a pipeline.
